2606.18246.title: Variable-Width Transformers
2606.18246.paper_summary: Scaling model size, specifically depth and width, has driven significant progress in transformer-based language models. However, most architectures maintain a constant width across all layers, allocating a fixed parameter and computation budget evenly despite different layers potentially playing distinct computational roles. In this work, we empirically investigate nonuniform capacity allocation across network depth by proposing a $\times$-shaped > <former architecture. This design maintains wider early and late layers while narrowing the middle layers, utilizing a parameter-free residual resizing mechanism. Across decoder-only language models ranging from 200M to 2B parameters (dense) and 3B parameters (MoE), our > <former consistently outperforms parameter-matched uniform baselines on language modeling loss. By reducing the average layer width, this architecture also requires fewer overall FLOPs (22% reduction under fitted loss-matched scaling curves) and smaller KV cache memory and I/O cost (15% reduction). In analysis, we show that this bottleneck structure results in qualitatively different representations in residual streams. Overall, our results demonstrate that nonuniform width allocation can result in more resource-optimal scaling of language models.
2606.18246.paper_summary_zh: 擴展模型的大小，特別是深度和寬度，推動了基於Transformer的語言模型的重大進展。<lb/>然而，大多數架構在所有層中保持恆定的寬度，儘管不同層可能扮演不同的計算角色，但仍然均勻分配固定的參數和計算預算。<lb/>在本研究中，我們通過提出一種 $\times$-形狀的 > <former 架構，實證研究了網絡深度中的非均勻容量分配。<lb/>這種設計在保持較寬的早期和晚期層的同時，縮小了中間層，利用無參數的殘差調整機制。<lb/>在從 200M 到 2B 參數（密集型）和 3B 參數（MoE）的僅解碼器語言模型中，我們的 > <former 在語言建模損失上始終優於參數匹配的均勻基準。<lb/>通過減少平均層寬度，這種架構還需要更少的總 FLOPs（在擬合損失匹配的縮放曲線下減少 22%）以及更小的 KV 緩存記憶體和 I/O 成本（減少 15%）。<lb/>在分析中，我們顯示這種瓶頸結構導致殘差流中的表示質量不同。<lb/>總體而言，我們的結果表明，非均勻寬度分配可以實現語言模型的更資源優化縮放。
2606.18246.author: Zhaofeng Wu et.al.
2606.18246.authors: Zhaofeng Wu, Oliver Sieberling, Shawn Tan, Rameswar Panda, Yury Polyanskiy, Yoon Kim
